ALL SPECIALTIES / SPEECH-LANGUAGE PATHOLOGIST / TENNESSEE

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Tennessee Speech-Language Pathologist License

Tennessee Speech-Language Pathologist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Speech-Language Pathologist applications in Tennessee run through Board of Communication Disorders and Sciences. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official graduate transcripts - From a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score report - Score of 162 or higher.
  • Clinical Fellowship verification - CF completion documentation.
  • Background check - TBI and FBI background check.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Tennessee is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.

  1. STEP 1

    Register and Apply via LARAS

    Navigate to the TN Department of Health Licensure and Regulatory System (LARAS) portal, register for a new account, and then log in to complete and submit the online application for Speech-Language Pathologist licensure.

    Fee: $165 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Submit Supporting Documentation

    Upload official transcripts showing a master's or higher degree in Speech-Language Pathology from an accredited institution, and documentation of supervised clinical experience (e.g., ASHA Clinical Fellowship Report) through the LARAS portal.

    Fee: $165 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Pass National Examination

    Arrange for your official scores from the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (Praxis 5331) to be sent directly to the Tennessee Board of Communication Disorders and Sciences.

    Fee: $165 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete Tennessee Jurisprudence Exam

    Take and pass the Tennessee Jurisprudence Examination for Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists, which can be accessed via CE Broker through a link provided on the Board's website.

    Fee: $165 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Undergo Criminal Background Check

    Complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check through IdentoGO, as instructed during the LARAS application process.

    Fee: $165 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Pay Application Fee

    Remit the required application fee of $175 as part of your submission within the LARAS portal.

    Fee: $165 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$50
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$165

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Board of Communication Disorders and Sciences
Phone
615-532-5136
Website

Application page
Open application

Renewal

Speech-Language Pathologist renewal in Tennessee follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$100
Continuing education
20 hours every 2 years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Tennessee SLP license cost?

$165 initial. $115 biennial renewal.

Is Tennessee part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes.

Does Tennessee allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes.

Does Tennessee have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

Yes. Tennessee participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Tennessee without a separate endorsement application.

How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Tennessee?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$165

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology

Compact

Yes

Renewal

Every 2 years · $100

Licensing board

Board of Communication Disorders and Sciences

tn.gov/health/health-program-areas/health-professional-boards/communication-disorders-board.html

This page is maintained as a reference for speech-language pathologists and recruiters evaluating Tennessee assignments.
